arts.ttl: Millwall attacker: I’m in a lot better place than last time by contract was running down
arts.sum: Duncan Watmore has admitted that his long-term Millwall future is still up in the air – but knows he is in a stronger position than when he was last inside the final year of his contract. Asked about his contract situation, Watmore told the South London Press : “I’m really enjoying it and I really like it here.

arts.ttl: Exclusive: Chris Rigg's transfer stance as Sunderland make decision over Manchester United target's future
arts.sum: The Red Devils want to sign the Black Cats prodigy.

arts.ttl: Millwall boss Harris chasing a hat-trick of high-profile Championship scalps at The Den
arts.sum: Harris told the South London Press : “We know Sunderland travel well, have attacking threat and they are top of the league for a reason – but we don’t fear anybody at The Den. Millwall were structurally excellent in 1-0 victories over Burnley and Leeds in SE16 while the 1-1 draw at Stoke City before the international break stretched their unbeaten run to seven league matches.

arts.ttl: Millwall boss quizzed on Bromley loanee’s future ahead of January transfer window
arts.sum: Neil Harris says Millwall are yet to make a call on Kamarl Grant’s Bromley loan future with the January transfer window on the horizon. “And in a lot of the games, he’s playing next to Byron Webster, another good Millwall man we know very well.

arts.ttl: Millwall boss has striker options dilemma after two players return to selection
arts.sum: Millwall boss Neil Harris says he now has the ability to adapt his side during a game after welcoming back strikers Tom Bradshaw and Josh Coburn from injury. Bradshaw made his first start after recovering from a hamstring injury he suffered in EFL Cup defeat to Leyton Orient in August but only played the first 45 minutes at the Bet 365 Stadium before being replaced by George Honeyman.

arts.ttl: ‘It’s always been the way at Millwall…it takes time’ - Millwall boss comments on summer signing’s growing influence
arts.sum: “It’s always been the way at our football club – it takes time,” said Harris, when asked about Azeez beginning to deliver strong displays. Millwall head coach Neil Harris has talked about the form of summer signing Femi Azeez.
